Container.Name: sure-aio
Container.Overview:
Sure (formerly Maybe Finance) is a self-hosted personal finance app for budgeting, net worth tracking, and account aggregation.
[b]All-In-One Unraid Edition[/b] sure-aio packages the web app, worker, PostgreSQL, and Redis into one Unraid template with persistent appdata paths and no external database setup required.
[b]First install[/b] 1. Generate a [code]SECRET_KEY_BASE[/code] with [code]openssl rand -hex 64[/code]. 2. Paste it below and click Apply. 3. Wait for first boot to finish provisioning the internal services.
[b]Advanced note[/b] External database, Redis, proxy, SMTP, AI, and storage overrides are available in Advanced View.
Container.ExtraSearchTerms: finance budgeting personal-finance net-worth money maybe-finance sure plaid bank accounts

Config.Name: Web UI Port
Config.Description: The main web interface port.

Config.Name: Secret Key Base
Config.Description: Critical: Run 'openssl rand -hex 64' in your Unraid terminal and paste the randomized hash here.

Config.Name: [Internal] Self Hosted Mode
Config.Description: Internal wrapper flag required for Sure self-hosted mode. Leave unchanged.

Config.Name: [Internal] Legacy Self Hosting Alias
Config.Description: Legacy upstream alias for self-hosted mode. Usually leave blank because SELF_HOSTED=true is already set by this wrapper.

Config.Name: App Volumes - Rails Storage
Config.Description: Internal rails file storage.

Config.Name: App Volumes - Postgres DB
Config.Description: Internal PostgreSQL database storage mapped externally so you don't lose data.

Config.Name: App Volumes - Redis Cache
Config.Description: Internal Redis memory cache.

Config.Name: [SSL] Custom CA Certificate Mount
Config.Description: Optional host path to a PEM CA certificate file for trusting self-signed or internal HTTPS services. Leave blank unless you need private CA support.

Config.Name: App Domain
Config.Description: The domain your Sure instance is hosted at (used for email links).

Config.Name: App URL
Config.Description: Optional full external base URL including scheme, such as 'https://finance.example.com'. Useful for advanced SSO flows that need an absolute callback or issuer URL.

Config.Name: Onboarding State
Config.Description: Controls user registration. Use 'open', 'closed', or 'invite_only'.

Config.Name: Require Invite Code
Config.Description: Optional global gate for account registration. Set to 'true' to require invite codes for sign-up.

Config.Name: Require Email Confirmation
Config.Description: Set to 'false' if you explicitly want to skip email confirmation for new accounts. Leave enabled for the safer default.

Config.Name: Assume SSL
Config.Description: Leave 'false' for direct LAN access. Set to 'true' only when Sure sits behind a SSL-terminating reverse proxy.

Config.Name: Force SSL Redirects
Config.Description: Leave 'false' for the default Unraid install over plain HTTP. Set to 'true' only if you want direct HTTP requests redirected to HTTPS.

Config.Name: [SSL] Custom CA File
Config.Description: Optional in-container path to a PEM CA certificate file. If you use the provided mount above, set this to '/certs/custom-ca.pem'.

Config.Name: [SSL] Override Global CA Bundle
Config.Description: Optional full CA bundle path for advanced Ruby/OpenSSL trust overrides. Usually leave blank and use SSL_CA_FILE instead.

Config.Name: [SSL] Verify Remote Certificates
Config.Description: Leave 'true' for production. Set to 'false' only for temporary testing against broken or self-signed HTTPS endpoints.

Config.Name: [SSL] Debug Logging
Config.Description: Set to 'true' to log detailed outbound SSL trust and certificate diagnostics.

Config.Name: [Legal] Privacy Policy URL
Config.Description: Optional external privacy-policy URL shown by the app when provided.

Config.Name: [Legal] Terms of Service URL
Config.Description: Optional external terms-of-service URL shown by the app when provided.

Config.Name: [External DB] DB Host Override
Config.Description: Optional external PostgreSQL host or container name. Example: '192.168.1.50' or 'postgres-shared' on a custom Docker network.

Config.Name: [External DB] DB Port Override
Config.Description: Optional external PostgreSQL port. Example: '5432'.

Config.Name: [External DB] DB Name Override
Config.Description: Optional external PostgreSQL database name. Leave blank to keep Sure's normal default database name.

Config.Name: [External DB] DB User Override
Config.Description: Optional external PostgreSQL username. This user must already exist on your external database.

Config.Name: [External DB] DB Password Override
Config.Description: Optional password for the external PostgreSQL user above.

Config.Name: [External DB] Redis URL Override
Config.Description: Optional external Redis URL. Example: 'redis://192.168.1.50:6379/1' or 'redis://:password@redis-host:6379/1'.

Config.Name: [External Redis] Sentinel Hosts
Config.Description: Optional Redis Sentinel hosts, comma-separated like 'host1:26379,host2:26379'. Takes precedence over REDIS_URL when set.

Config.Name: [External Redis] Sentinel Master
Config.Description: Redis Sentinel master name.

Config.Name: [External Redis] Sentinel Username
Config.Description: Redis Sentinel username if your Sentinel deployment requires authentication.

Config.Name: [External Redis] Sentinel Password
Config.Description: Redis password used for Sentinel-backed Redis deployments.

Config.Name: [System] Product Name
Config.Description: Custom product name in UI.

Config.Name: [System] Brand Name
Config.Description: Custom brand name in UI.

Config.Name: [System] Default UI Layout
Config.Description: Choose the initial layout for new sessions. Use 'dashboard' for the standard app or 'intro' for the intro-first experience.

Config.Name: [DB Encryption] Primary Key
Config.Description: Optional explicit Rails encryption primary key. Leave blank unless you deliberately manage separate Active Record encryption keys outside SECRET_KEY_BASE.

Config.Name: [DB Encryption] Deterministic Key
Config.Description: Optional deterministic encryption key paired with the primary key above. Leave blank unless you already know your Rails encryption key material.

Config.Name: [DB Encryption] Derivation Salt
Config.Description: Optional key-derivation salt for Rails encryption. Leave blank unless you manage custom encryption keys yourself.

Config.Name: [AI] OpenAI / Ollama Token
Config.Description: OpenAI-compatible API key. Get OpenAI keys from platform.openai.com/api-keys. If using local Ollama, enter any non-empty placeholder such as 'ollama-local'.

Config.Name: [AI] OpenAI URI Base
Config.Description: Leave blank for official OpenAI. For local LLMs, enter your endpoint (e.g., 'http://ollama:11434/v1').

Config.Name: [AI] Model Name
Config.Description: If using Ollama, you MUST define the model here (e.g., 'llama3.1:13b' or 'gemma2:7b').

Config.Name: [AI] Categorization Provider
Config.Description: Optional provider override used only for transaction categorization. Example: 'openai' or 'ollama'. If blank, Sure uses its normal AI provider behavior.

Config.Name: [AI] Categorization Model
Config.Description: Optional model override used only for categorization, such as 'gemma2:7b'.

Config.Name: [AI] Chat Provider
Config.Description: Optional provider override used only for chat-assistant requests. Example: 'openai' or 'ollama'.

Config.Name: [AI] Chat Model
Config.Description: Optional model override used only for chat-assistant requests, such as 'gpt-4.1' or a local Ollama model.

Config.Name: [AI] Request Timeout
Config.Description: OpenAI-compatible request timeout in seconds. Raise this only if your provider or local LLM is slow to respond.

Config.Name: [AI] JSON Mode Override
Config.Description: Optional structured-output override. Valid values are '', 'strict', 'none', or 'json_object'. Set this only if you need to force Sure's OpenAI JSON behavior globally.

Config.Name: [AI] Debug Logging
Config.Description: Set to 'true' to enable verbose AI chat debugging in logs.

Config.Name: [AI] Enable PDF Processing
Config.Description: Leave 'true' for OpenAI or vision-capable providers. Set to 'false' only for OpenAI-compatible endpoints that do not support PDF or vision input.

Config.Name: [AI] Vector Store Provider
Config.Description: Optional document-search backend. Leave blank for the default path. Set to 'pgvector' to use PostgreSQL-based vectors or 'qdrant' for an external Qdrant server.

Config.Name: [AI] Embedding Model
Config.Description: Embedding model name used for document search. Example: 'nomic-embed-text'. This is required when you enable pgvector or qdrant-backed document search.

Config.Name: [AI] Embedding Dimensions
Config.Description: Embedding width for the selected model. Must match the provider output.

Config.Name: [AI] Embedding URI Base
Config.Description: Optional dedicated embeddings endpoint. Example: 'http://ollama:11434/v1'. If blank, Sure falls back to OPENAI_URI_BASE.

Config.Name: [AI] Embedding Access Token
Config.Description: Optional dedicated embeddings token. If blank, Sure falls back to OPENAI_ACCESS_TOKEN.

Config.Name: [AI] Qdrant URL
Config.Description: Optional external Qdrant endpoint for vector storage. Example: 'http://192.168.1.50:6333'.

Config.Name: [AI] Qdrant API Key
Config.Description: Optional Qdrant API key from your Qdrant Cloud or self-hosted auth configuration.

Config.Name: [Ext. AI] Assistant Type
Config.Description: Set to 'external' to route all chat to an external agent via MCP.

Config.Name: [Ext. AI] Assistant URL
Config.Description: URL for the external agent (e.g. https://your-openclaw/v1/chat/completions).

Config.Name: [Ext. AI] Assistant Token
Config.Description: Auth token expected by your external agent or gateway. Copy it from that service's dashboard or config.

Config.Name: [Ext. AI] Agent ID
Config.Description: Optional Agent ID for OpenClaw routing.

Config.Name: [Ext. AI] Session Key
Config.Description: Optional stable session key for remote agent conversation persistence.

Config.Name: [Ext. AI] Allowed Emails
Config.Description: Optional comma-separated allowlist of users permitted to use the external assistant.

Config.Name: [Ext. AI] MCP User Email
Config.Description: Required if using Ext. AI: Email of an existing Sure user.

Config.Name: [Ext. AI] MCP API Token
Config.Description: Required if using Ext. AI: Bearer token for agent callbacks to /mcp. Generate one with 'openssl rand -hex 32' in the Unraid terminal.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] PostHog Key
Config.Description: PostHog project API key from your PostHog project settings.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] PostHog Host
Config.Description: PostHog host URL. Example: 'https://us.i.posthog.com' or your self-hosted PostHog URL.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Langfuse Host
Config.Description: Langfuse base URL for LLM observability. Example: 'https://cloud.langfuse.com' or your self-hosted Langfuse URL.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Langfuse Region
Config.Description: Optional Langfuse region shortcut like 'us' or 'eu'. Use this only if you are not setting a custom Langfuse Host URL.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Langfuse Public Key
Config.Description: Langfuse public key from your project settings.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Langfuse Secret Key
Config.Description: Langfuse secret key from your project settings.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Sentry DSN
Config.Description: Optional Sentry DSN if you want upstream exception reporting enabled for this instance.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Skylight Enabled
Config.Description: Set to 'true' only if you intentionally use Skylight's hosted APM service. Default is 'false' for AIO installs so no external Skylight setup is required.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Skylight Auth Token
Config.Description: Optional Skylight app authentication token. Only used when SKYLIGHT_ENABLED is true and you want to send APM data to your Skylight account.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Logtail API Key
Config.Description: Optional Better Stack / Logtail source token from your log source settings.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Logtail Ingest Host
Config.Description: Optional Logtail ingest host used with LOGTAIL_API_KEY.

Config.Name: [Telemetry] Rails Log Level
Config.Description: Application log verbosity. Use 'info' for normal operation or 'debug' for deeper troubleshooting.

Config.Name: [Runtime] Rails/Sidekiq Thread Pool
Config.Description: Optional worker thread count used by Puma, Sidekiq, and DB pool sizing. Leave blank for upstream default (3).

Config.Name: [Runtime] Puma Worker Processes
Config.Description: Optional Puma process count for the web service. Leave blank for upstream default (1).

Config.Name: [Runtime] Sidekiq Web Username
Config.Description: Optional username for /sidekiq dashboard basic auth. Leave blank to keep upstream default username ('sure').

Config.Name: [Runtime] Sidekiq Web Password
Config.Description: Optional password for /sidekiq dashboard basic auth. Leave blank to keep upstream default password ('sure').

Config.Name: [Network] HTTPS Proxy
Config.Description: Optional outbound HTTPS proxy URL (for advanced egress controls like Pipelock). Leave blank for normal direct outbound traffic.

Config.Name: [Network] HTTP Proxy
Config.Description: Optional outbound HTTP proxy URL. Leave blank unless your network requires a proxy.

Config.Name: [Network] No Proxy Hosts
Config.Description: Optional comma-separated hosts/domains that should bypass HTTP(S) proxy routing.

Config.Name: [API] Exchange Rate Provider
Config.Description: Optional exchange-rate provider override. If left blank, Sure uses its normal default and UI selection behavior.

Config.Name: [API] Securities Provider
Config.Description: Optional securities provider override. If left blank, Sure uses its normal default and UI selection behavior.

Config.Name: [API] Brandfetch Client ID
Config.Description: Brandfetch client ID from your Brandfetch application or dashboard if you want merchant and bank logos.

Config.Name: [API] Brandfetch High-Res Logos
Config.Description: Optional env override for 120x120 Brandfetch logos. Set to 'true' to force high-res logos, 'false' to force standard size. Leave blank to keep the in-app toggle enabled.

Config.Name: [API] Indexa API Token
Config.Description: Optional global API token used by the Indexa Capital provider when account-level credentials are not configured.

Config.Name: [API] Twelve Data Key
Config.Description: Optional Twelve Data API key from twelvedata.com if you want exchange rates or securities from Twelve Data instead of Yahoo Finance.

Config.Name: [API] Twelve Data URL Override
Config.Description: Optional custom Twelve Data API base URL. Leave blank unless you are routing Twelve Data through a proxy or alternate endpoint.

Config.Name: [API] Yahoo Finance URL Override
Config.Description: Optional custom Yahoo Finance API base URL. Leave blank for normal public Yahoo Finance access.

Config.Name: [API] Yahoo Finance Max Retries
Config.Description: Maximum retry attempts for Yahoo Finance requests before Sure gives up.

Config.Name: [API] Yahoo Finance Retry Interval
Config.Description: Seconds to wait between Yahoo Finance retry attempts.

Config.Name: [API] Yahoo Finance Min Request Interval
Config.Description: Optional minimum spacing between Yahoo Finance requests in seconds. Leave blank to keep upstream defaults.

Config.Name: [Sync] Auto Sync Enabled
Config.Description: Container-level override for Sure's scheduled sync job. Use '1' to keep it enabled or '0' to disable automatic syncs globally.

Config.Name: [Sync] Auto Sync Time
Config.Description: Daily auto-sync time in 24-hour HH:MM format.

Config.Name: [Sync] Auto Sync Timezone
Config.Description: Timezone used with AUTO_SYNC_TIME. Example: 'America/Denver'.

Config.Name: [Sync] SimpleFIN Include Pending
Config.Description: Set to '0' to exclude pending SimpleFIN transactions. If set here, upstream disables the corresponding Sync setting in the Sure UI.

Config.Name: [Sync] SimpleFIN Raw Debug Logs
Config.Description: Set to 'true' to log raw SimpleFIN payloads for debugging. This can expose sensitive data and create noisy logs.

Config.Name: [Sync] SimpleFIN Credit Overpayment Heuristic
Config.Description: Optional override for SimpleFIN liability overpayment detection. Set to 'false' to disable the heuristic globally.

Config.Name: [Sync] Plaid Include Pending
Config.Description: Set to '0' to exclude pending Plaid transactions. If set here, upstream disables the corresponding Sync setting in the Sure UI.

Config.Name: [Sync] Lunchflow Include Pending
Config.Description: Set to 'true' to include pending transactions in Lunchflow sync requests.

Config.Name: [Sync] Lunchflow Raw Debug Logs
Config.Description: Set to 'true' to log raw Lunchflow payloads for debugging. This can expose sensitive data and create noisy logs.

Config.Name: [Auth] Local Login Enabled
Config.Description: Set to 'false' to disable local email/password login and move users toward SSO-only auth.

Config.Name: [Auth] Local Admin Override
Config.Description: If local login is disabled, set to 'true' to let super admins keep local login as an emergency backdoor.

Config.Name: [Auth] JIT SSO Mode
Config.Description: SSO behavior for first-time users: 'create_and_link' creates accounts automatically, 'link_only' requires an existing user.

Config.Name: [Auth] Allowed OIDC Domains
Config.Description: Optional comma-separated email domains allowed for JIT SSO account creation.

Config.Name: [Auth] Provider Source
Config.Description: Leave blank for normal YAML/env-backed provider loading. Set to 'db' if you want upstream's database-backed SSO provider admin UI.

Config.Name: [Auth] OIDC Client ID
Config.Description: OIDC client ID from your identity provider app registration, such as Authentik, Authelia, Keycloak, or Zitadel.

Config.Name: [Auth] OIDC Client Secret
Config.Description: OIDC client secret from the same identity provider app registration.

Config.Name: [Auth] OIDC Issuer
Config.Description: OIDC issuer URL. Example: 'https://auth.example.com/application/o/sure/' or your provider's issuer endpoint.

Config.Name: [Auth] OIDC Redirect URI
Config.Description: OIDC redirect URI registered with your provider. Example: 'https://finance.example.com/auth/openid_connect/callback'.

Config.Name: [Auth] OIDC Button Label
Config.Description: Optional custom sign-in button label for the default OIDC provider.

Config.Name: [Auth] OIDC Button Icon
Config.Description: Optional icon slug for the default OIDC sign-in button.

Config.Name: [Auth] Google OAuth Client ID
Config.Description: Optional Google OAuth client ID from console.cloud.google.com if you want a dedicated Google sign-in provider.

Config.Name: [Auth] Google OAuth Client Secret
Config.Description: Optional Google OAuth client secret from the same Google OAuth app.

Config.Name: [Auth] Google Button Label
Config.Description: Optional custom label for the Google sign-in button.

Config.Name: [Auth] Google Button Icon
Config.Description: Optional icon slug for the Google sign-in button.

Config.Name: [Auth] GitHub OAuth Client ID
Config.Description: Optional GitHub OAuth client ID from your GitHub OAuth App settings if you want a dedicated GitHub sign-in provider.

Config.Name: [Auth] GitHub OAuth Client Secret
Config.Description: Optional GitHub OAuth client secret from the same GitHub OAuth App.

Config.Name: [Auth] GitHub Button Label
Config.Description: Optional custom label for the GitHub sign-in button.

Config.Name: [Auth] GitHub Button Icon
Config.Description: Optional icon slug for the GitHub sign-in button.

Config.Name: [Auth:Keycloak] Client ID
Config.Description: Optional named Keycloak OIDC provider client ID for upstream multi-provider auth.yml mode.

Config.Name: [Auth:Keycloak] Client Secret
Config.Description: Optional named Keycloak OIDC provider client secret for upstream multi-provider auth.yml mode.

Config.Name: [Auth:Keycloak] Issuer
Config.Description: Optional named Keycloak OIDC issuer URL for upstream multi-provider auth.yml mode.

Config.Name: [Auth:Keycloak] Redirect URI
Config.Description: Optional named Keycloak OIDC redirect URI for upstream multi-provider auth.yml mode.

Config.Name: [Auth:Authentik] Client ID
Config.Description: Optional named Authentik OIDC provider client ID for upstream multi-provider auth.yml mode.

Config.Name: [Auth:Authentik] Client Secret
Config.Description: Optional named Authentik OIDC provider client secret for upstream multi-provider auth.yml mode.

Config.Name: [Auth:Authentik] Issuer
Config.Description: Optional named Authentik OIDC issuer URL for upstream multi-provider auth.yml mode.

Config.Name: [Auth:Authentik] Redirect URI
Config.Description: Optional named Authentik OIDC redirect URI for upstream multi-provider auth.yml mode.

Config.Name: [Storage] Provider Strategy
Config.Description: Leave blank for internal disk storage. Change to 'amazon', 'cloudflare', or 'generic_s3' to move uploads out of the container.

Config.Name: [Storage:AWS] Access Key ID
Config.Description: Amazon S3 access key ID from your AWS IAM user or access-key pair.

Config.Name: [Storage:AWS] Secret Access Key
Config.Description: Amazon S3 secret access key paired with the access key ID above.

Config.Name: [Storage:AWS] Region
Config.Description: Amazon S3 region. Defaults to us-east-1 if left blank.

Config.Name: [Storage:AWS] Bucket Name
Config.Description: Amazon S3 bucket name.

Config.Name: [Storage:R2] Cloudflare Account ID
Config.Description: Cloudflare account ID used to construct the R2 endpoint URL.

Config.Name: [Storage:R2] Access Key ID
Config.Description: Cloudflare R2 access key ID from your R2 API token pair.

Config.Name: [Storage:R2] Secret Access Key
Config.Description: Cloudflare R2 secret access key paired with the R2 access key ID above.

Config.Name: [Storage:R2] Bucket Name
Config.Description: Cloudflare R2 bucket name.

Config.Name: [Storage:Generic S3] Access Key ID
Config.Description: Generic S3 or MinIO access key ID from your object-storage service.

Config.Name: [Storage:Generic S3] Secret Access Key
Config.Description: Generic S3 or MinIO secret access key paired with the access key ID above.

Config.Name: [Storage:Generic S3] Region
Config.Description: Generic S3 region value expected by your provider.

Config.Name: [Storage:Generic S3] Bucket Name
Config.Description: Generic S3 or MinIO bucket name.

Config.Name: [Storage:Generic S3] Custom Endpoint
Config.Description: Custom MinIO or S3-compatible endpoint URL.

Config.Name: [Storage:Generic S3] Force Path Style
Config.Description: Set to 'true' for providers that require path-style S3 requests.

Config.Name: [Email] SMTP Address
Config.Description: Hostname for your SMTP server. Example: 'smtp.mailgun.org', 'smtp.sendgrid.net', or your mail relay host.

Config.Name: [Email] SMTP Port
Config.Description: Port for your SMTP server. Common values: '465' for implicit TLS or '587' for STARTTLS.

Config.Name: [Email] SMTP Username
Config.Description: SMTP username from your mail provider or relay.

Config.Name: [Email] SMTP Password
Config.Description: SMTP password or app password from your mail provider.

Config.Name: [Email] SMTP TLS Enabled
Config.Description: Leave 'true' for normal secure SMTP. Set to 'false' only if your mail relay expects plain SMTP without TLS.

Config.Name: [Email] Sender Address
Config.Description: The email address your app will send mail from (e.g., finance@mydomain.com).

Config.Name: [Plaid] Client ID
Config.Description: Optional Plaid client ID if you want upstream Plaid account linking enabled.

Config.Name: [Plaid] Secret
Config.Description: Optional Plaid secret paired with the client ID above.

Config.Name: [Plaid] Environment
Config.Description: Optional Plaid environment such as 'sandbox' or 'production'.

Config.Name: [Plaid EU] Client ID
Config.Description: Optional Plaid Europe client ID if you use the Plaid EU adapter.

Config.Name: [Plaid EU] Secret
Config.Description: Optional Plaid Europe secret paired with the Plaid EU client ID above.

Config.Name: [Plaid EU] Environment
Config.Description: Optional Plaid Europe environment such as 'sandbox' or 'production'.
